governing_principle: SuperSXO makes the search-to-action journey visible inside the interface itself.
journey_statement: Search Intent → Visibility → Experience → Trust → Navigation → Action → Strategic Outcome

layers.label: Search Intent
layers.definition: The original motivation, question, or goal that drives a user to initiate a search. The pre-search mental state that determines what kind of result will satisfy the user.
layers.interface_expression: Diagnostic entry points, intent-mapping visualizations, and query-to-purpose indicators that reveal what the user was looking for before arriving.
layers.diagnostic_relevance: Understanding intent determines whether a page was designed to match the right signal. Misaligned intent is the first failure point in the search-to-action journey.

layers.label: Visibility
layers.definition: The degree to which the asset appears in search results for relevant intent signals. Includes ranking, snippet quality, structured data presentation, and AI search inclusion.
layers.interface_expression: Signal path indicators, visibility score layers, and search presence mapping within the spatial interface.
layers.diagnostic_relevance: Visibility is the entry condition for the journey. Without visibility, the remainder of the journey cannot begin. However, visibility alone does not constitute success.

layers.label: Experience
layers.definition: The quality of the interaction a user has with the page after arrival. Includes load performance, interface clarity, content relevance, and structural coherence.
layers.interface_expression: Experience quality panels, diagnostic grid indicators for page speed and structural clarity, and content-to-intent alignment maps.
layers.diagnostic_relevance: A poor experience breaks the journey immediately after visibility succeeds. Experience failures are measurable through Core Web Vitals, engagement signals, and structural audits.

layers.label: Trust
layers.definition: The user's assessment of whether the site is credible, authoritative, and safe to engage with. Trust is built through content quality, visual professionalism, clear authorship, and institutional tone.
layers.interface_expression: Trust architecture plane within the spatial interface. Authority signals, institutional design language, and structured credential indicators.
layers.diagnostic_relevance: Trust is the most commonly missed layer in standard SEO approaches. A site can rank and load quickly but fail to generate trust, causing users to exit before action.

layers.label: Navigation
layers.definition: The user's ability to move through the site with confidence, finding what they need without confusion or dead ends. Navigation includes internal link quality, menu clarity, and path predictability.
layers.interface_expression: Navigation confidence layer showing path clarity, internal link maps, and route completion indicators within the control plane.
layers.diagnostic_relevance: Navigation failure causes drop-off before conversion. Users who trust the site but cannot navigate it will not take action. Navigation is both a UX and an SEO diagnostic dimension.

layers.label: Action
layers.definition: The specific, measurable response taken by the user: form submission, tool use, content consumption, purchase, or request. Action is the measurable outcome of a successful search-to-action journey.
layers.interface_expression: Action path indicators, conversion signal maps, and CTA clarity layers in the diagnostic interface. Clear, single-intent call-to-action design.
layers.diagnostic_relevance: Action is the terminal point that determines whether search investment has yielded any real return. All prior layers exist to make action possible and likely.

layers.label: Strategic Outcome
layers.definition: The downstream business, strategic, or asset-building consequence of repeated successful search-to-action journeys. For SuperSXO, this includes authority building, recurring income, and acquisition positioning.
layers.interface_expression: Strategic outcome observatory within the spatial interface. Long-horizon metrics, authority trajectory indicators, and acquisition readiness signals.
layers.diagnostic_relevance: Strategic outcome separates sovereign asset thinking from ordinary website thinking. The goal is not a single conversion but a durable, compounding strategic result.
